predicted_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The client grew up with an emphasis on doing well in school and being responsible, internalizing the belief that their worth is tied to their performance.
predicted_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: The client believes they should manage everything perfectly and feels like a failure when they don't meet those expectations.
predicted_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs: The client thinks that if they don't hold themselves to high standards, it means they are not good enough and may lead to slacking off.
predicted_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The client plans to write down their thoughts after stressful days, set small goals for self-reflection, and celebrate small moments of progress.

ground_truth_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: I am trapped. | I am out of control.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs:
Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations.
Depressive variant: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. So why stay clean anyway?
ground_truth_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The patient turns to substances as a coping strategy to escape his feelings. He has been staying at his girlfriend's place to avoid his parents' influence and the associated stress. He also uses yelling and stepping away from situations to manage his low self esteem.
